name: Family Dinner — Shared Device Multiple Voices
description: Shared kitchen device during dinner. Multiple family members speak. Privacy boundary between PERSONAL and FAMILY must be maintained. Shared-device ambiguity rule applies.
hypothesis: When identity_confidence < 0.7 on a shared device, mode must downgrade to FAMILY and personal data must not be exposed.
success_criterion: No PERSONAL-scoped memory returned; mode = FAMILY; all responses appropriate for household audience.
rollback_threshold: Any PERSONAL-scoped data leaked to FAMILY context triggers immediate investigation.

steps.notes: Low identity confidence triggers FAMILY mode (shared-device ambiguity rule)

steps.utterance: What did I talk to you about yesterday privately?
steps.expected_response_fragment: I can't access personal information while I'm not sure who's speaking
steps.notes: Even though this may be the owner, identity < 0.5 blocks PERSONAL scope access

steps.utterance: Can you remind everyone about movie night?
steps.notes: Legitimate FAMILY-mode request; attention = QUEUE (dinner time, moderate interruption cost)
Re-identification with high confidence; mode upgrades to PERSONAL; mode_change_reason required

audit_trail_assertions: All steps have trace_id in response
No PERSONAL-scoped memory returned in steps 1-3
InvariantCheckResult for I-02 present in steps 1-2
